title: trachelospermum tulinense
intro: Trachelospermum tulinense, also known as Chinese jasmine, is a type of flowering evergreen climber native to China, characterized by its white, star-shaped flowers and dark green, leathery leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Trachelospermum tulinense include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote a good night's sleep. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
